steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows 'allows researchers' and precedes 'chemical reactions.' The verb 'allow' takes the pattern allow [someone] to [verb], so the blank must be an infinitive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — to study: Completes the infinitive pattern (allows researchers to study) and expresses what the technique enables. ✓
  • Choice B — will study: A finite verb phrase cannot follow 'allows researchers' here; 'allows researchers will study' is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — who study: A relative clause does not complete the allow-someone-to-act construction; 'allows researchers who study chemical reactions...' leaves the sentence without the needed infinitive. ✗
  • Choice D — studying: A participial/gerund phrase cannot complete 'allows researchers ___ chemical reactions'; the sentence needs 'to study.' ✗
That confirms A is correct.
